Party_Details.Name: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd was incorporated to organize conventions and events.
The plaintiff purchased the property at 51A/B Neil Road for $2,400,000.
A valuation report valued the property at $2,050,000.
Payment of $42,000 was made to the first defendant as director’s fees.
Payment of $58,200 was made to Your Holiday Planners Pte Ltd.
Payment of $510,000 was divided among shareholders.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the directors of a company can be held legally responsible for actions that allegedly contravened their management obligations.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act in the best interest of the company and ensure that all transactions are conducted with transparency and proper authorization.
Party_Details.Application: The plaintiff stated that the first defendant acted without proper board approval in selling the property and distributing funds. The defendants argued that all actions were ratified by the shareholders.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the plaintiff's claims were unsubstantiated and that the transactions were ratified by the shareholders.

Party_Details.Name: Lee Khiam Long

Facts.Text: Lee Khiam Long was the managing director of Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd.
Lee Khiam Long received $42,000 as director’s fees.
Resolutions were passed to sell the property and distribute funds.
Completion of the sale of the property to the second and third defendants.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be held legally responsible for actions taken in the management of company assets without explicit board approval.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must ensure that all significant company transactions are approved by the board or shareholders to avoid contravening management obligations.
Party_Details.Application: The defendant argued that the sale and distribution of funds were approved by the shareholders. The plaintiff stated that these actions were unauthorized.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court concluded that the actions were ratified by the shareholders and dismissed the claims against the defendant.

Party_Details.Name: Lee Khiam Long

Facts.Text: Lee Khiam Long claimed $111,122.25 from the plaintiff.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can claim reimbursement for expenses incurred in the course of company management.
Party_Details.Rule: A director is entitled to claim legitimate expenses incurred in the course of fulfilling their management duties.
Party_Details.Application: The plaintiff by counterclaim stated that the expenses were legitimate and supported by documentation. The defendant by counterclaim did not challenge the claim.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepted the evidence provided by the plaintiff by counterclaim and allowed the claim for reimbursement.

Party_Details.Name: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd was claimed against for $111,122.25 by Lee Khiam Long.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company is legally responsible to reimburse a director for claimed expenses.
Party_Details.Rule: A company is obligated to reimburse directors for legitimate expenses incurred in the course of their duties, provided proper documentation is submitted.
Party_Details.Application: The defendant by counterclaim did not provide evidence to refute the claim. The plaintiff by counterclaim provided documentation supporting the expenses.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found the claim to be valid and ordered the company to reimburse the claimed amount.
